title: The Bench Watcher
text_zh: 公园里有一位老人，每天下午两点准时坐在同一张长椅上，什么也不做，只是安静地看着来来往往的人。我第一次注意到他的时候，以为他在等什么人，但连续观察了两个星期以后，我发现他从来不跟任何人打招呼，也不看手机，甚至不带一本书。他只是坐在那里，用一种极其平静的目光注视着眼前的一切。有一天我忍不住坐到了他旁边，试着用他的视角去看这个公园。我发现了很多以前从未注意到的细节：遛狗的人其实分成两类——牵着绳子被狗带着走的，和把绳子放得很松让狗自由探索的；跑步的人经过喷泉时几乎都会不自觉地偏头看一眼水面；推婴儿车的年轻父母走到树荫下会自动放慢脚步。这些微小的行为模式像一幅隐形的地图，铺展在公园的每一条小路上。我在那张长椅上坐了整整一个下午，第一次体会到「什么也不做」其实是一种被严重低估的能力——它让世界主动向你展开，而不是你去追赶世界。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí wèi lǎorén, měi tiān xiàwǔ liǎng diǎn zhǔnshí zuò zài tóng yì zhāng chángyǐ shàng, shénme yě bú zuò, zhǐ shì ānjìng de kàn zhe láilái wǎngwǎng de rén. Wǒ dì yī cì zhùyì dào tā de shíhou, yǐwéi tā zài děng shénme rén, dàn liánxù guānchá le liǎng gè xīngqī yǐhòu, wǒ fāxiàn tā cónglái bù gēn rènhé rén dǎ zhāohu, yě bú kàn shǒujī, shènzhì bú dài yì běn shū. Tā zhǐ shì zuò zài nàlǐ, yòng yì zhǒng jíqí píngjìng de mùguāng zhùshì zhe yǎnqián de yíqiè. Yǒu yì tiān wǒ rěn bú zhù zuò dào le tā pángbiān, shì zhe yòng tā de shìjiǎo qù kàn zhège gōngyuán. Wǒ fāxiàn le hěn duō yǐqián cónɡ wèi zhùyì dào de xìjié: liù gǒu de rén qíshí fēn chéng liǎng lèi — qiān zhe shéngzi bèi gǒu dài zhe zǒu de, hé bǎ shéngzi fàng de hěn sōng ràng gǒu zìyóu tànsuǒ de; pǎobù de rén jīngguò pēnquán shí jīhū dōu huì bù zìjué de piān tóu kàn yì yǎn shuǐmiàn; tuī yīng'ér chē de niánqīng fùmǔ zǒu dào shùyīn xià huì zìdòng fàng màn jiǎobù. Zhèxiē wēixiǎo de xíngwéi móshì xiàng yì fú yǐnxíng de dìtú, pūzhǎn zài gōngyuán de měi yì tiáo xiǎo lù shàng. Wǒ zài nà zhāng chángyǐ shàng zuò le zhěngzhěng yí gè xiàwǔ, dì yī cì tǐhuì dào 「shénme yě bú zuò」 qíshí shì yì zhǒng bèi yánzhòng dīgū de nénglì — tā ràng shìjiè zhǔdòng xiàng nǐ zhǎnkāi, ér búshì nǐ qù zhuīgǎn shìjiè.
text_en: In the park there's an old man who sits on the same bench at exactly two o'clock every afternoon, doing nothing, just quietly watching the people come and go. When I first noticed him, I thought he was waiting for someone, but after observing for two consecutive weeks, I found he never greets anyone, never looks at his phone, doesn't even bring a book. He just sits there, watching everything before him with an extraordinarily calm gaze. One day I couldn't help sitting down next to him, trying to see the park from his perspective. I discovered many details I'd never noticed before: dog walkers actually fall into two types — those holding tight leashes being led by their dogs, and those with slack leashes letting dogs explore freely; runners passing the fountain almost all unconsciously tilt their heads to glance at the water surface; young parents pushing strollers automatically slow down when they reach tree shade. These tiny behavioral patterns are like an invisible map, unfolding along every path in the park. I sat on that bench for an entire afternoon, and for the first time understood that 'doing nothing' is actually a severely underestimated ability — it lets the world unfold toward you, rather than you chasing after the world.

questions.q_zh: 作者在长椅上观察到跑步的人经过喷泉时有什么习惯？
questions.q_en: What habit did the author observe in runners passing the fountain?

options.text: 不自觉地偏头看一眼水面
options.pinyin: bù zìjué de piān tóu kàn yì yǎn shuǐmiàn
options.text_en: Unconsciously tilt their heads to glance at the water

questions.q_zh: 作者认为「什么也不做」是一种什么样的能力？
questions.q_en: What kind of ability does the author consider 'doing nothing' to be?

options.text_en: A bad habit that wastes time

options.text: 只有老年人才有的习惯
options.pinyin: zhǐyǒu lǎoniánrén cái yǒu de xíguàn
options.text_en: A habit only elderly people have

title: The Language of Rain on Windows
text_zh: 下雨天我喜欢坐在窗边不开灯，听雨水敲打玻璃的声音。大多数人把雨声当作一种均匀的白噪音，但如果你真正集中注意力去听，每一场雨其实都有自己的节奏和个性。春天的细雨像是有人用指尖轻轻地、犹豫不决地点着窗户，声音细碎而断续；夏天的暴雨则像是整面墙被水帘包裹，单个雨滴的声音完全被淹没在巨大的轰鸣中；秋天的雨最有层次感，大滴和小滴交替落下，像一段即兴演奏的鼓点。最让我着迷的是雨滴击中窗户不同位置时发出的不同音调。窗框的金属部分会发出清脆的「叮」声，玻璃中央是沉闷的「咚」，而窗台的石材表面则产生一种短促的「啪」。这三种声音以随机的顺序组合在一起，构成了一首永远不会重复的即兴曲。我曾经试着用文字记录雨的节奏变化，但很快发现这种尝试本身就违背了倾听的本质——真正的倾听不是为了记录或分析，而是为了让自己暂时消融在声音之中，成为雨的一部分。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān wǒ xǐhuan zuò zài chuāng biān bù kāi dēng, tīng yǔshuǐ qiāodǎ bōli de shēngyīn. Dà duōshù rén bǎ yǔ shēng dàng zuò yì zhǒng jūnyún de bái zàoyīn, dàn rúguǒ nǐ zhēnzhèng jízhōng zhùyìlì qù tīng, měi yì chǎng yǔ qíshí dōu yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu hé gèxìng. Chūntiān de xì yǔ xiàng shì yǒu rén yòng zhǐjiān qīngqīng de, yóuyù bú jué de diǎn zhe chuānghù, shēngyīn xìsuì ér duànxù; xiàtiān de bàoyǔ zé xiàng shì zhěng miàn qiáng bèi shuǐlián bāoguǒ, dān gè yǔdī de shēngyīn wánquán bèi yānmò zài jùdà de hōngmíng zhōng; qiūtiān de yǔ zuì yǒu céngcì gǎn, dà dī hé xiǎo dī jiāotì luò xià, xiàng yí duàn jíxìng yǎnzòu de gǔdiǎn. Zuì ràng wǒ zháomí de shì yǔdī jī zhòng chuānghù bùtóng wèizhì shí fāchū de bùtóng yīndiào. Chuāngkuàng de jīnshǔ bùfen huì fāchū qīngcuì de 「dīng」 shēng, bōli zhōngyāng shì chénmèn de 「dōng」, ér chuāngtái de shícái biǎomiàn zé chǎnshēng yì zhǒng duǎncù de 「pā」. Zhè sān zhǒng shēngyīn yǐ suíjī de shùnxù zǔhé zài yìqǐ, gòuchéng le yì shǒu yǒngyuǎn bú huì chóngfù de jíxìng qǔ. Wǒ céngjīng shì zhe yòng wénzì jìlù yǔ de jiézòu biànhuà, dàn hěn kuài fāxiàn zhè zhǒng chángshì běnshēn jiù wéibèi le qīngtīng de běnzhì — zhēnzhèng de qīngtīng búshì wèile jìlù huò fēnxī, ér shì wèile ràng zìjǐ zànshí xiāoróng zài shēngyīn zhī zhōng, chéngwéi yǔ de yí bùfen.
text_en: On rainy days I like to sit by the window without turning on the light, listening to rain tapping against the glass. Most people treat rain as a uniform white noise, but if you truly concentrate and listen, every rainfall actually has its own rhythm and personality. Spring drizzle is like someone tentatively, hesitantly tapping the window with a fingertip, the sound fine and intermittent; summer downpour is like an entire wall wrapped in a water curtain, individual raindrops completely drowned in the enormous rumble; autumn rain has the most depth, large drops and small drops falling in alternation, like an improvised drum pattern. What fascinates me most is the different tones raindrops produce hitting different spots on the window. The metal window frame makes a crisp 'ding,' the center of the glass produces a muffled 'dong,' and the stone windowsill surface creates a short, sharp 'pa.' These three sounds combine in random sequences, composing an improvisation that will never repeat. I once tried to record the rain's rhythmic changes in writing, but quickly discovered that this attempt itself violated the essence of listening — true listening isn't for recording or analysis, but for temporarily dissolving yourself into the sound, becoming part of the rain.

questions.q_zh: 作者认为秋天的雨有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What characteristic does the author attribute to autumn rain?

options.text: 最有层次感，大小滴交替
options.pinyin: zuì yǒu céngcì gǎn, dà xiǎo dī jiāotì
options.text_en: Most layered, large and small drops alternating

questions.q_zh: 作者为什么放弃了用文字记录雨声？
questions.q_en: Why did the author give up recording rain sounds in writing?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǔ shēng tài fùzá le
options.text_en: Because the rain sounds were too complex

options.text: 因为笔记本被雨淋湿了
options.pinyin: yīnwèi bǐjìběn bèi yǔ lín shī le

options.text: 因为记录违背了倾听的本质
options.pinyin: yīnwèi jìlù wéibèi le qīngtīng de běnzhì
options.text_en: Because recording violates the essence of listening

options.pinyin: yīnwèi yǔ hěn kuài jiù tíng le

title: The Geometry of Shadows
text_zh: 冬天的下午，阳光很低，影子被拉得很长。我发现自己开始有意识地观察各种事物投下的影子，并且注意到一个有趣的现象：影子往往比它的主体更能揭示事物的本质形态。一棵冬天落光了叶子的树，它的影子投在人行道上，变成了一幅精确的血管分布图——每一根枝条的分叉角度、粗细变化都被忠实地记录在地面上。一个骑自行车的人经过，影子先于他到达，像是他未来几秒钟的预演。最令人沉思的是建筑物的影子。一栋普通的居民楼在下午三点钟投下的阴影恰好覆盖了对面人行道上的三张长椅，到了四点钟，阴影向东移动，释放了第一张长椅，有人立刻坐上去晒太阳。这些无意识的选择每天都在发生——人们追随着光影的边界移动，却很少意识到自己正在参与一场由太阳和建筑共同编排的缓慢舞蹈。我在日记中画下了不同时刻影子的轮廓，几个月以后翻看，这些记录比任何照片都更精确地保存了季节流转的痕迹。
text_pinyin: Dōngtiān de xiàwǔ, yángguāng hěn dī, yǐngzi bèi lā de hěn cháng. Wǒ fāxiàn zìjǐ kāishǐ yǒu yìshí de guānchá gè zhǒng shìwù tóu xià de yǐngzi, bìngqiě zhùyì dào yí gè yǒuqù de xiànxiàng: yǐngzi wǎngwǎng bǐ tā de zhǔtǐ gèng néng jiēshì shìwù de běnzhì xíngtài. Yì kē dōngtiān luò guāng le yèzi de shù, tā de yǐngzi tóu zài rénxíngdào shàng, biàn chéng le yì fú jīngquè de xuèguǎn fēnbù tú — měi yì gēn zhītiáo de fēnchā jiǎodù, cūxì biànhuà dōu bèi zhōngshí de jìlù zài dìmiàn shàng. Yí gè qí zìxíngchē de rén jīngguò, yǐngzi xiān yú tā dàodá, xiàng shì tā wèilái jǐ miǎozhōng de yùyǎn. Zuì lìng rén chénsī de shì jiànzhùwù de yǐngzi. Yí dòng pǔtōng de jūmín lóu zài xiàwǔ sān diǎn zhōng tóu xià de yīnyǐng qiàhǎo fùgài le duìmiàn rénxíngdào shàng de sān zhāng chángyǐ, dào le sì diǎn zhōng, yīnyǐng xiàng dōng yídòng, shìfàng le dì yī zhāng chángyǐ, yǒu rén lìkè zuò shàngqù shài tàiyáng. Zhèxiē wú yìshí de xuǎnzé měi tiān dōu zài fāshēng — rénmen zhuīsuí zhe guāngyǐng de biānjiè yídòng, què hěn shǎo yìshí dào zìjǐ zhèngzài cānyù yì chǎng yóu tàiyáng hé jiànzhù gòngtóng biānpái de huǎnmàn wǔdǎo. Wǒ zài rìjì zhōng huà xià le bùtóng shíkè yǐngzi de lúnkuò, jǐ gè yuè yǐhòu fān kàn, zhèxiē jìlù bǐ rènhé zhàopiàn dōu gèng jīngquè de bǎocún le jìjié liúzhuǎn de hénjì.
text_en: On winter afternoons, the sunlight hangs low and shadows stretch very long. I found myself beginning to consciously observe the shadows cast by various things, and noticed an interesting phenomenon: shadows often reveal an object's essential form better than the object itself. A tree stripped of leaves in winter casts its shadow on the sidewalk, becoming a precise vascular distribution map — every branch's forking angle and thickness variation faithfully recorded on the ground. A cyclist passes, their shadow arriving before them, like a preview of the next few seconds. Most thought-provoking are the shadows of buildings. An ordinary apartment building's shadow at three o'clock in the afternoon happens to cover three benches on the opposite sidewalk; by four o'clock the shadow shifts east, releasing the first bench, and someone immediately sits down to bask in the sun. These unconscious choices happen every day — people follow the boundary of light and shadow, yet rarely realize they're participating in a slow dance choreographed jointly by the sun and buildings. I sketched the outlines of shadows at different moments in my journal, and months later when I looked back, these records preserved traces of the passing seasons more precisely than any photograph.

questions.q_zh: 作者认为冬天落叶树的影子像什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author compare the shadow of a bare winter tree to?

options.text: 一幅精确的血管分布图
options.pinyin: yì fú jīngquè de xuèguǎn fēnbù tú
yì kē dào guòlái de shù

questions.q_zh: 人们在光影变化中有什么无意识的行为？
questions.q_en: What unconscious behavior do people exhibit in response to changing light and shadow?

options.text_en: Following the boundary of light and shadow

title: The Night Librarian's Hours
title_zh: 夜班图书管理员的时间
text_zh: 大学图书馆的夜班从晚上十点持续到凌晨两点。作为值夜班的图书管理员，我逐渐发现了一些只有在深夜才会浮现的规律。十点到十一点是「挣扎时段」，学生们不断在座位上调整坐姿，频繁起身去接水或上厕所，阅读进度几乎停滞。这是注意力和疲劳感交战的时刻。十一点以后，一种奇特的安静开始蔓延。留下来的学生进入了某种深层的专注状态，翻书的频率变得稳定而有节奏，像一台调好了转速的机器。偶尔有人抬起头，目光空洞地望着天花板几秒钟，然后又低下头继续阅读——这个动作在深夜的图书馆里反复出现，我把它叫做「思维换气」。凌晨一点左右，最有趣的现象出现了：剩下的七八个学生之间产生了一种无声的默契。有人去接水的时候会顺便帮旁边的人也带一杯，没有任何语言交流，只有一个微小的点头。这些互不相识的人在深夜的图书馆中形成了一个临时的、脆弱的共同体，被同一种安静的坚持连接在一起。
text_pinyin: Dàxué túshūguǎn de yèbān cóng wǎnshang shí diǎn chíxù dào língchén liǎng diǎn. Zuòwéi zhí yèbān de túshū guǎnlǐyuán, wǒ zhújiàn fāxiàn le yìxiē zhǐyǒu zài shēnyè cái huì fúxiàn de guīlǜ. Shí diǎn dào shíyī diǎn shì 「zhēngzhá shíduàn」, xuéshēngmen búduàn zài zuòwèi shàng tiáozhěng zuòzī, pínfán qǐshēn qù jiē shuǐ huò shàng cèsuǒ, yuèdú jìndù jīhū tíngzhì. Zhè shì zhùyìlì hé píláo gǎn jiāozhàn de shíkè. Shíyī diǎn yǐhòu, yì zhǒng qítè de ānjìng kāishǐ mànyán. Liú xiàlái de xuéshēng jìnrù le mǒu zhǒng shēncéng de zhuānzhù zhuàngtài, fān shū de pínlǜ biàn de wěndìng ér yǒu jiézòu, xiàng yì tái tiáo hǎo le zhuǎnsù de jīqì. Ǒu'ěr yǒu rén tái qǐ tóu, mùguāng kōngdòng de wàng zhe tiānhuābǎn jǐ miǎozhōng, ránhòu yòu dī xià tóu jìxù yuèdú — zhège dòngzuò zài shēnyè de túshūguǎn lǐ fǎnfù chūxiàn, wǒ bǎ tā jiào zuò 「sīwéi huàn qì」. Língchén yì diǎn zuǒyòu, zuì yǒuqù de xiànxiàng chūxiàn le: shèng xià de qī bā gè xuéshēng zhī jiān chǎnshēng le yì zhǒng wúshēng de mòqì. Yǒu rén qù jiē shuǐ de shíhou huì shùnbiàn bāng pángbiān de rén yě dài yì bēi, méiyǒu rènhé yǔyán jiāoliú, zhǐyǒu yí gè wēixiǎo de diǎn tóu. Zhèxiē hù bù xiāngshí de rén zài shēnyè de túshūguǎn zhōng xíngchéng le yí gè línshí de, cuìruò de gòngtóngtǐ, bèi tóng yì zhǒng ānjìng de jiānchí liánjiē zài yìqǐ.
text_en: The university library's night shift runs from ten at night until two in the morning. As the night-shift librarian, I gradually discovered patterns that only emerge in the deep hours. Ten to eleven is the 'struggle period' — students constantly adjust their posture in their seats, frequently get up to fill water or use the restroom, reading progress nearly stalls. This is the moment when attention and fatigue are at war. After eleven, a peculiar quiet begins to spread. The remaining students enter some deep state of focus, their page-turning frequency becoming steady and rhythmic, like a machine set to the right speed. Occasionally someone raises their head, stares vacantly at the ceiling for a few seconds, then lowers their head to continue reading — this action repeats throughout the late-night library, and I call it 'thought breathing.' Around one in the morning, the most interesting phenomenon appears: among the remaining seven or eight students, a silent understanding forms. When someone goes to fill water, they'll bring back a cup for the person beside them too, with no verbal exchange, just a tiny nod. These strangers in the late-night library have formed a temporary, fragile community, connected by the same quiet persistence.

questions.q_zh: 作者所说的「思维换气」是什么动作？
questions.q_en: What action does the author call 'thought breathing'?

options.text: 抬头望天花板几秒然后继续阅读
options.pinyin: tái tóu wàng tiānhuābǎn jǐ miǎo ránhòu jìxù yuèdú
options.text_en: Raising head to stare at ceiling a few seconds then continuing to read

questions.q_zh: 凌晨一点左右，剩下的学生之间产生了什么？
questions.q_en: What forms among the remaining students around 1 AM?

options.text: 无声的默契和临时共同体
options.pinyin: wúshēng de mòqì hé línshí gòngtóngtǐ
options.text_en: A silent understanding and temporary community

title: Reading Hands at a Tea House
text_zh: 老茶馆里喝茶的时候，我养成了一个习惯——不看人的脸，而是观察他们的手。手比表情更诚实，因为大多数人不知道如何控制自己手部的微小动作。坐在我对面的那位商人，说话时面部表情始终保持着自信的微笑，但他的右手一直在无意识地撕杯垫的边缘，暴露了他内心的紧张。角落里的老夫妻已经不怎么说话了，但丈夫每次给自己倒茶的时候都会先摸一下妻子杯子的温度，如果凉了就先给她续上——这个动作如此自然，以至于他自己可能都不知道自己在做。最让我触动的是一位独自喝茶的中年女性。她的手指反复抚摸着茶杯的边缘，动作极其缓慢，仿佛在通过触觉确认某种存在。她的手腕上戴着两只手表，一只走着，一只停了。我无法确定那只停止的手表意味着什么，但它让我联想到了某种关于时间和失去的故事。手是无声的叙述者，它们讲述的故事比语言更加细腻，更加不容篡改。
text_pinyin: Lǎo cháguǎn lǐ hē chá de shíhou, wǒ yǎngchéng le yí gè xíguàn — bú kàn rén de liǎn, ér shì guānchá tāmen de shǒu. Shǒu bǐ biǎoqíng gèng chéngshí, yīnwèi dà duōshù rén bù zhīdào rúhé kòngzhì zìjǐ shǒu bù de wēixiǎo dòngzuò. Zuò zài wǒ duìmiàn de nà wèi shāngrén, shuōhuà shí miànbù biǎoqíng shǐzhōng bǎochí zhe zìxìn de wēixiào, dàn tā de yòu shǒu yìzhí zài wú yìshí de sī bēi diàn de biānyuán, bàolù le tā nèixīn de jǐnzhāng. Jiǎoluò lǐ de lǎo fūqī yǐjīng bù zěnme shuōhuà le, dàn zhàngfu měi cì gěi zìjǐ dào chá de shíhou dōu huì xiān mō yíxià qīzi bēizi de wēndù, rúguǒ liáng le jiù xiān gěi tā xù shàng — zhège dòngzuò rúcǐ zìrán, yǐzhìyú tā zìjǐ kěnéng dōu bù zhīdào zìjǐ zài zuò. Zuì ràng wǒ chùdòng de shì yí wèi dúzì hē chá de zhōngnián nǚxìng. Tā de shǒuzhǐ fǎnfù fǔmō zhe chábēi de biānyuán, dòngzuò jíqí huǎnmàn, fǎngfú zài tōngguò chùjué quèrèn mǒu zhǒng cúnzài. Tā de shǒuwàn shàng dài zhe liǎng zhī shǒubiǎo, yì zhī zǒu zhe, yì zhī tíng le. Wǒ wúfǎ quèdìng nà zhī tíngzhǐ de shǒubiǎo yìwèi zhe shénme, dàn tā ràng wǒ liánxiǎng dào le mǒu zhǒng guānyú shíjiān hé shīqù de gùshi. Shǒu shì wúshēng de xùshù zhě, tāmen jiǎngshù de gùshi bǐ yǔyán gèngjiā xìnì, gèngjiā bùróng cuàngǎi.
text_en: When drinking tea at the old teahouse, I developed a habit — instead of watching people's faces, I observe their hands. Hands are more honest than facial expressions, because most people don't know how to control the tiny movements of their hands. The businessman sitting across from me maintained a confident smile throughout the conversation, but his right hand kept unconsciously tearing at the edge of the coaster, betraying his inner nervousness. The elderly couple in the corner had mostly stopped talking, but every time the husband poured tea for himself, he'd first touch his wife's cup to check the temperature, refilling hers first if it had gone cool — this gesture was so natural that he himself probably didn't realize he was doing it. What moved me most was a middle-aged woman drinking tea alone. Her fingers repeatedly traced the rim of her teacup, the motion extremely slow, as though confirming some existence through touch. On her wrist she wore two watches — one running, one stopped. I couldn't determine what the stopped watch meant, but it made me think of some story about time and loss. Hands are silent narrators, and the stories they tell are more delicate than language, more resistant to falsification.

questions.q_zh: 老夫妻中丈夫有什么无意识的习惯？
questions.q_en: What unconscious habit does the husband of the elderly couple have?

options.text_en: Choosing the type of tea for his wife

Because the author is a palm reader

title: The Sound Map of Early Morning
text_zh: 每天凌晨五点醒来以后，我会在床上闭着眼睛躺十分钟，不做任何事情，只是倾听窗外的声音。经过一年多的练习，我现在可以仅凭声音精确地判断时间，误差不超过十五分钟。五点整的时候，世界几乎是无声的，只有远处偶尔传来一两声鸟叫——那是最早醒来的麻雀在试探性地发出第一声鸣叫。五点十分左右，第一辆公共汽车从小区门前经过，发动机的声音在空旷的街道上传播得异常清晰。五点半，楼下早餐店的铁卷门「哗啦」一声拉开，紧接着是金属锅铲碰撞炒锅的声音。五点四十五分，遛狗的脚步声开始出现——清晨的脚步声和白天不一样，节奏更慢，似乎人和狗在这个时间段都还带着一种半梦半醒的迟缓。到了六点，声音的密度突然增加：更多的车辆，更多的脚步，偶尔夹杂着孩子不情愿的哭声。城市像一台精密的仪器，每天以几乎相同的顺序启动各个部件。而我躺在这台仪器的中心，闭着眼睛，感受它一点一点地从沉睡中醒来。
text_pinyin: Měi tiān língchén wǔ diǎn xǐng lái yǐhòu, wǒ huì zài chuáng shàng bì zhe yǎnjīng tǎng shí fēnzhōng, bú zuò rènhé shìqing, zhǐ shì qīngtīng chuāng wài de shēngyīn. Jīngguò yì nián duō de liànxí, wǒ xiànzài kěyǐ jǐn píng shēngyīn jīngquè de pànduàn shíjiān, wùchā bù chāoguò shíwǔ fēnzhōng. Wǔ diǎn zhěng de shíhou, shìjiè jīhū shì wúshēng de, zhǐyǒu yuǎnchù ǒu'ěr chuán lái yì liǎng shēng niǎo jiào — nà shì zuì zǎo xǐng lái de máquè zài shìtàn xìng de fāchū dì yī shēng míngjiào. Wǔ diǎn shí fēn zuǒyòu, dì yī liàng gōnggòng qìchē cóng xiǎoqū mén qián jīngguò, fādòngjī de shēngyīn zài kōngkuàng de jiēdào shàng chuánbō de yìcháng qīngxī. Wǔ diǎn bàn, lóu xià zǎocān diàn de tiě juǎn mén 「huālā」 yì shēng lā kāi, jǐn jiē zhe shì jīnshǔ guōchǎn pèngzhuàng chǎo guō de shēngyīn. Wǔ diǎn sìshíwǔ fēn, liù gǒu de jiǎobù shēng kāishǐ chūxiàn — qīngchén de jiǎobù shēng hé báitiān bù yíyàng, jiézòu gèng màn, sìhū rén hé gǒu zài zhège shíjiān duàn dōu hái dài zhe yì zhǒng bàn mèng bàn xǐng de chíhuǎn. Dào le liù diǎn, shēngyīn de mìdù tūrán zēngjiā: gèng duō de chēliàng, gèng duō de jiǎobù, ǒu'ěr jiāzá zhe háizi bù qíngyuàn de kū shēng. Chéngshì xiàng yì tái jīngmì de yíqì, měi tiān yǐ jīhū xiāngtóng de shùnxù qǐdòng gè gè bùjiàn. Ér wǒ tǎng zài zhè tái yíqì de zhōngxīn, bì zhe yǎnjīng, gǎnshòu tā yìdiǎn yìdiǎn de cóng chénshuì zhōng xǐng lái.
text_en: Every day after waking at five in the morning, I lie in bed with eyes closed for ten minutes, doing nothing, just listening to the sounds outside the window. After more than a year of practice, I can now judge the time precisely by sound alone, with an error of no more than fifteen minutes. At exactly five, the world is nearly silent, with only an occasional bird call from afar — the earliest sparrows tentatively issuing their first cries. Around five-ten, the first bus passes the compound entrance, its engine sound spreading with unusual clarity through the empty streets. At five-thirty, the metal rolling shutter of the breakfast shop downstairs clangs open, immediately followed by the sound of a metal spatula striking a wok. At five forty-five, dog-walking footsteps begin to appear — early morning footsteps differ from daytime ones, the rhythm slower, as though both person and dog still carry a half-dreaming sluggishness. By six, the density of sound suddenly increases: more vehicles, more footsteps, occasionally interspersed with a child's reluctant crying. The city is like a precision instrument, starting its various components each day in nearly the same sequence. And I lie at the center of this instrument, eyes closed, feeling it wake from sleep bit by bit.

options.text: 节奏更慢，带着半梦半醒的迟缓
options.pinyin: jiézòu gèng màn, dài zhe bàn mèng bàn xǐng de chíhuǎn
options.text_en: Slower rhythm, carrying a half-dreaming sluggishness

options.text: 一台精密仪器按顺序启动
options.pinyin: yì tái jīngmì yíqì àn shùnxù qǐdòng

options.pinyin: yì tiáo huǎnmàn liúdòng de hé

title: Moss Cartography
text_zh: 搬到这个老小区以后，我开始注意到一种大多数人视而不见的存在——苔藓。它们生长在墙根、台阶的缝隙、废弃花盆的边缘、水龙头下方的水泥地面上。苔藓是世界上最安静的定居者，它们不发出任何声音，不散发明显的气味，不会移动，也不会长得很大，却以一种极其顽固的方式占据着被所有其他生命忽略的领地。通过长期的观察，我发现苔藓的分布其实是一张精确的微气候地图。它们标记出了建筑物的排水路线——凡是雨水经常流过的地方，苔藓必定更加茂盛。它们揭示了日照的死角——那些全年都照不到阳光的墙面裂缝里，苔藓形成了厚厚的绿色绒毯。它们甚至记录了人的行为痕迹：经常有人站着抽烟的墙角，地面的苔藓被鞋底反复碾压，形成了一圈明显的「秃斑」。我有时候蹲在某一小片苔藓前面观察很久，想象这些微小的植物如何在没有任何人关注的情况下，用几十年的时间缓慢地绘制出一座城市的隐藏肖像。它们不需要被看见，不需要被理解，只是安静地存在着，像是这个喧嚣世界最忠实的见证者。
text_pinyin: Bān dào zhège lǎo xiǎoqū yǐhòu, wǒ kāishǐ zhùyì dào yì zhǒng dà duōshù rén shì ér bú jiàn de cúnzài — táixiǎn. Tāmen shēngzhǎng zài qiánggēn, táijiē de fèngxì, fèiqì huāpén de biānyuán, shuǐlóngtóu xiàfāng de shuǐní dìmiàn shàng. Táixiǎn shì shìjiè shàng zuì ānjìng de dìngjū zhě, tāmen bù fāchū rènhé shēngyīn, bú sànfā míngxiǎn de qìwèi, bú huì yídòng, yě bú huì zhǎng de hěn dà, què yǐ yì zhǒng jíqí wángù de fāngshì zhànjù zhe bèi suǒyǒu qítā shēngmìng hūlüè de lǐngdì. Tōngguò chángqī de guānchá, wǒ fāxiàn táixiǎn de fēnbù qíshí shì yì zhāng jīngquè de wēi qìhòu dìtú. Tāmen biāojì chū le jiànzhùwù de páishuǐ lùxiàn — fánshì yǔshuǐ jīngcháng liú guò de dìfang, táixiǎn bìdìng gèngjiā màoshèng. Tāmen jiēshì le rìzhào de sǐjiǎo — nàxiē quán nián dōu zhào bú dào yángguāng de qiángmiàn lièfèng lǐ, táixiǎn xíngchéng le hòu hòu de lǜsè róngtǎn. Tāmen shènzhì jìlù le rén de xíngwéi hénjì: jīngcháng yǒu rén zhàn zhe chōuyān de qiángjiǎo, dìmiàn de táixiǎn bèi xiédǐ fǎnfù niǎnyā, xíngchéng le yì quān míngxiǎn de 「tūbān」. Wǒ yǒu shíhou dūn zài mǒu yì xiǎo piàn táixiǎn qiánmiàn guānchá hěn jiǔ, xiǎngxiàng zhèxiē wēixiǎo de zhíwù rúhé zài méiyǒu rènhé rén guānzhù de qíngkuàng xià, yòng jǐ shí nián de shíjiān huǎnmàn de huìzhì chū yí zuò chéngshì de yǐncáng xiàoxiàng. Tāmen bù xūyào bèi kàn jiàn, bù xūyào bèi lǐjiě, zhǐ shì ānjìng de cúnzài zhe, xiàng shì zhège xuānxiāo shìjiè zuì zhōngshí de jiànzhèng zhě.
text_en: After moving to this old residential compound, I began noticing an existence that most people are blind to — moss. It grows at the base of walls, in the cracks of steps, at the edges of abandoned flowerpots, on the cement surface beneath faucets. Moss is the world's quietest settler — it makes no sound, emits no obvious scent, doesn't move, doesn't grow very large, yet occupies territory ignored by all other life with extraordinary stubbornness. Through long-term observation, I discovered that the distribution of moss is actually a precise microclimate map. It marks out buildings' drainage paths — wherever rainwater frequently flows, moss is invariably more lush. It reveals the blind spots of sunlight — in wall cracks that receive no sunlight all year, moss forms thick green carpets. It even records traces of human behavior: at wall corners where someone regularly stands to smoke, the ground moss has been repeatedly crushed by shoe soles, forming a conspicuous 'bald patch.' Sometimes I crouch before a small patch of moss and observe for a long time, imagining how these tiny plants, with no one paying them any attention, slowly draft a city's hidden portrait over decades. They don't need to be seen, don't need to be understood, they simply exist quietly, like the most faithful witnesses of this noisy world.

questions.q_zh: 苔藓在经常有人抽烟的墙角有什么特征？
questions.q_en: What characteristic does moss have at wall corners where people regularly smoke?

options.text_en: Crushed by shoe soles forming a 'bald patch'

questions.q_zh: 作者认为苔藓的分布实际上反映了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author believe moss distribution actually reflects?

options.text: 微气候、排水路线和人的行为痕迹
options.pinyin: wēi qìhòu, páishuǐ lùxiàn hé rén de xíngwéi hénjì
options.text_en: Microclimate, drainage paths, and traces of human behavior

title: The Patience of Drying Laundry
text_zh: 阳台上晾着的衣服教会了我关于时间的另一种理解方式。把刚洗完的衣服挂到晾衣架上以后，如果你每隔几分钟去摸一次，你会发现衣服在很长一段时间内似乎没有任何变化——它始终是湿的。这让人产生一种错觉，以为干燥的过程根本没有发生。但如果你忍住不去触摸它，过了三四个小时再去看，衣服已经完全干了。变化一直在发生，只是在你能感知的阈值之下。我觉得这和很多重要的事情是一样的：学习一门语言、修复一段关系、理解自己——所有真正深层的转变都发生在意识的雷达之下，无法被实时监控。过于频繁地检查进度反而会产生「什么都没改变」的焦虑。最理想的状态是像晾衣服一样：做好该做的准备——清洗、拧干、展开、挂好，然后把它交给时间和空气，去做别的事情。当你再次想起来的时候，一切已经在你不知道的时刻悄然完成了。这种对缓慢过程的信任，也许是现代生活中最稀缺的一种智慧。
text_pinyin: Yángtái shàng liàng zhe de yīfu jiāohuì le wǒ guānyú shíjiān de lìng yì zhǒng lǐjiě fāngshì. Bǎ gāng xǐ wán de yīfu guà dào liàng yī jià shàng yǐhòu, rúguǒ nǐ měi gé jǐ fēnzhōng qù mō yí cì, nǐ huì fāxiàn yīfu zài hěn cháng yí duàn shíjiān nèi sìhū méiyǒu rènhé biànhuà — tā shǐzhōng shì shī de. Zhè ràng rén chǎnshēng yì zhǒng cuòjué, yǐwéi gānzào de guòchéng gēnběn méiyǒu fāshēng. Dàn rúguǒ nǐ rěn zhù bú qù chùmō tā, guò le sān sì gè xiǎoshí zài qù kàn, yīfu yǐjīng wánquán gān le. Biànhuà yìzhí zài fāshēng, zhǐ shì zài nǐ néng gǎnzhī de yùzhí zhī xià. Wǒ juéde zhè hé hěn duō zhòngyào de shìqing shì yíyàng de: xuéxí yì mén yǔyán, xiūfù yí duàn guānxi, lǐjiě zìjǐ — suǒyǒu zhēnzhèng shēncéng de zhuǎnbiàn dōu fāshēng zài yìshí de léidá zhī xià, wúfǎ bèi shíshí jiānkòng. Guòyú pínfán de jiǎnchá jìndù fǎn'ér huì chǎnshēng 「shénme dōu méi gǎibiàn」 de jiāolǜ. Zuì lǐxiǎng de zhuàngtài shì xiàng liàng yīfu yíyàng: zuò hǎo gāi zuò de zhǔnbèi — qīngxǐ, nǐng gān, zhǎnkāi, guà hǎo, ránhòu bǎ tā jiāo gěi shíjiān hé kōngqì, qù zuò bié de shìqing. Dāng nǐ zàicì xiǎng qǐlái de shíhou, yíqiè yǐjīng zài nǐ bù zhīdào de shíkè qiǎorán wánchéng le. Zhè zhǒng duì huǎnmàn guòchéng de xìnrèn, yěxǔ shì xiàndài shēnghuó zhōng zuì xīquē de yì zhǒng zhìhuì.
text_en: The laundry drying on the balcony taught me another way of understanding time. After hanging freshly washed clothes on the drying rack, if you touch them every few minutes, you'll find the clothes seem unchanged for a long time — they're still wet. This creates an illusion that the drying process hasn't happened at all. But if you resist touching them and come back three or four hours later, the clothes are completely dry. Change has been happening all along, just below your threshold of perception. I think this is the same with many important things: learning a language, repairing a relationship, understanding yourself — all truly deep transformations occur beneath the radar of consciousness, impossible to monitor in real time. Checking progress too frequently actually produces the anxiety of 'nothing has changed.' The ideal state is like drying laundry: do the necessary preparation — wash, wring, spread, hang — then entrust it to time and air, and go do something else. When you think of it again, everything has been quietly completed at a moment you didn't know. This trust in slow processes is perhaps the scarcest wisdom in modern life.

questions.q_zh: 作者认为频繁检查衣服干了没有会导致什么？
questions.q_en: What does the author believe frequently checking if clothes are dry leads to?

options.text: 产生「什么都没改变」的焦虑
options.pinyin: chǎnshēng 「shénme dōu méi gǎibiàn」 de jiāolǜ

questions.q_zh: 作者把晾衣服的道理类比到了哪些事情？
questions.q_en: What things does the author draw an analogy to from the laundry lesson?

options.text: 学语言、修复关系、理解自己
options.pinyin: xué yǔyán, xiūfù guānxi, lǐjiě zìjǐ
options.text_en: Learning a language, repairing relationships, understanding oneself
